entries.name: Ohori Park Cycling Loop
entries.description: Cruise around the scenic 2.7 km lake circuit at Ohori Park on a rental bike. The flat loop circles a large central pond framed by weeping willows and traditional torii — a rare pocket of calm inside a busy city. Best tackled early morning before crowds arrive.
notes: Bike rentals available at the park entrance (~1,000–1,500 ¥/hr, no booking needed)
The loop is flat and takes 20–30 minutes at a relaxed pace — do multiple laps
Go early (before 9 AM) for cool temperatures and near-empty paths
Ohori Park is a 5-min taxi or 15-min walk from Tenjin subway station

entries.name: Naka River Water Bus Cruise
entries.description: Glide along the Naka River on a 40–60 minute water bus cruise through Fukuoka's urban waterfront. The route passes Nakasu island, riverside yatai stalls, and views of the PayPay Dome and Fukuoka Tower. A perfect palate cleanser between ramen stops.
notes: Departs from docks near Hakata Port — walk-up tickets available
Late afternoon slot (3–5 PM) offers softer light and cooler air
May is ideal — mild temps and no rain before tsuyu season
Pairs naturally with the evening yatai stalls at Nakasu, a short walk from the dock

days.title: Universal Studios Japan - One Piece Day

entries.name: One Piece at Universal Studios Japan
entries.description: One Piece Premier Summer attractions and shows at USJ — dedicated One Piece area with themed rides and Sanji's Pirate Restaurant
notes: Seasonal One Piece events run during summer months
Arrive before park opening for shortest wait times
BUY EXPRESS PASS — worth every yen for popular rides
Download USJ app for real-time wait times

entries.name: Fushimi Inari Trail
entries.description: Hike through thousands of vermillion torii gates winding up the mountain behind Fushimi Inari Taisha — free and open 24/7
notes: 2-4 hours depending on pace and how far up you go
Less crowded as you climb higher past the main shrine
Early morning or late afternoon for best photos
Small shrines and fox statues scattered along the trail

entries.name: Mt. Kurama to Kibune Hike
entries.description: A 2-hour forest and temple hike connecting the mountain village of Kurama to the riverside town of Kibune — one of Kyoto's best half-day hikes
notes: Start from Kurama Station (Eizan Railway from Demachiyanagi)
Trail passes through Kurama-dera temple grounds
Finish in Kibune for riverside dining (kawadoko) in summer
Bring water — limited vending machines on the trail

entries.name: Old Tokaido Road in Hakone
entries.description: Historic samurai trail through cedar forests — walk the same stone-paved road Edo-period travelers used between Kyoto and Tokyo
notes: Can visit en route from Kyoto to Tokyo via Hakone
1.5 hours from Tokyo by train
Combine with Hakone hot springs for a soak after hiking
Well-preserved stone path through towering cedar trees

entries.name: Mt. Takao Hike
entries.description: One of Tokyo's most accessible mountain hikes — about 1 hour from Shinjuku with multiple trail options from easy to challenging
notes: Take Keio Line from Shinjuku to Takaosanguchi (~50 min)
Trail 1 is paved and easy; Trail 6 follows a stream through forest
Cable car available for part of the ascent
Excellent half-day trip — summit views of Mt. Fuji on clear days

entries.name: Pokemon Cafe
entries.description: Themed food and drinks at the official Pokemon Cafe near Tokyo Station — Pikachu-shaped pancakes, character latte art, and exclusive merchandise
notes: Reservations open 1 month before — book immediately when slots open
Located inside Pokemon Center Tokyo DX near Tokyo Station
Meals are themed around different Pokemon each season
Gift shop inside cafe has exclusive items not sold elsewhere
entries.booking_note: Opens 1 month ahead — sells out same day

entries.name: Ghibli Museum
entries.description: Immersive museum dedicated to Studio Ghibli films in Mitaka — designed by Hayao Miyazaki himself with exclusive short films, exhibits, and a rooftop garden
notes: Tickets only via Lawson machines or online lottery — no walk-ins
Book 1 month ahead when tickets become available
About 20 minutes from Shinjuku on the JR Chuo Line
Worth it if you love Spirited Away, Totoro, or any Ghibli film
entries.booking_note: Lottery tickets only — book 1 month ahead via Lawson

entries.name: Akihabara Anime Shopping
entries.description: Tokyo's legendary electronics and anime district — visit Animate, Mandarake Complex, Radio Kaikan, and Kotobukiya for anime goods, figures, and retro games
notes: Mandarake Complex has 8 floors of used anime, manga, and figures
Radio Kaikan is a multi-floor building packed with specialty shops
Hit the arcades — SEGA and Taito Station for crane games and rhythm games
Best prices on figures and collectibles compared to other cities

entries.name: Nakano Broadway
entries.description: Less crowded alternative to Akihabara with excellent vintage anime goods and the Mandarake flagship store — a hidden gem for serious collectors
notes: Take JR Chuo Line from Shinjuku — just 5 minutes
Mandarake flagship occupies multiple floors here
Better selection of vintage and rare items than Akihabara
Walk through the covered Nakano Sun Mall to reach it
